title: Chest pain safety-net documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports documenting concerns, uncertainty, questions, explanation of reviewed tests, shared decisions, and clinician-provided advice for recurrent pain without generating patient instructions.
assessment.rationale: The NICE section supports recording concerns, uncertainty, reviewed-investigation explanation, questions, and patient-specific recurrent-pain advice only when actually discussed by a clinician.

source.title: Recent-onset chest pain of suspected cardiac origin: assessment and diagnosis
source.population: Adults with recent-onset acute or stable chest pain or discomfort suspected to be cardiac in origin.
source.setting: Initial, urgent, primary, outpatient, and specialist assessment of recent-onset chest pain.
source.exclusions: Exact for clinician-documented chest-pain history, associated symptoms, risk context, examination and reviewed tests, information, and safety-net discussion. It is old and only partial for atypical or established non-acute follow-up; diagnosis, urgency, testing, treatment, and referral decisions are not generated.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1.1-1.1.1.9 - information, uncertainty, test discussion, and recurrent-pain advice

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting concerns, uncertainty, questions, explanation of reviewed tests, shared decisions, and clinician-provided advice for recurrent pain without generating patient instructions.

assessment.text: Lists life-threatening chest-pain patterns, associated breathlessness, syncope, diaphoresis, abnormal physiology, and emergency referral requirements.
assessment.rationale: The DHA section supports documenting assessed high-risk symptoms, abnormal observations, and clinician escalation concern as prompts; it does not generate an emergency conclusion or instruction.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Chest Pain – 45
source.population: People presenting with chest pain; no precise age range is stated in the applicability section.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth chest-pain documentation; partial for a setting-agnostic GP workflow and emergency presentations excluded from telehealth.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Lists life-threatening chest-pain patterns, associated breathlessness, syncope, diaphoresis, abnormal physiology, and emergency referral requirements.

sources_used.title: Recent-onset chest pain of suspected cardiac origin: assessment and diagnosis
DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Chest Pain – 45

additions.final_wording: Supports documenting concerns, uncertainty, questions, explanation of reviewed tests, shared decisions, and clinician-provided advice for recurrent pain without generating patient instructions.
